q.en: On September 11, 2001, how many planes were hijacked?
q.zh: 在2001年9月11日，共有多少架飞机被劫持？

explain.en: The book says, 'a group of terrorists hijacked four planes on the East Coast.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“一群恐怖分子在东海岸劫持了四架飞机。”

q.en: Where did the fourth plane crash?
q.zh: 第四架飞机坠毁在哪里？

options.zh: 宾夕法尼亚州的一片田野

options.en: Into the World Trade Center according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 撞入世贸中心（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says people on board stopped the fourth plane, and 'That plane crashed in a field in Pennsylvania.'
explain.zh: 书中说，机上乘客阻止了第四架飞机，它“坠毁在宾夕法尼亚州的一片田野”。

options.en: To repair a vehicle according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 修理交通工具（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To seize control of a vehicle by outside force
options.zh: 由外力夺取对交通工具的控制权

explain.en: The glossary defines hijacked as 'seized control of a vehicle by outside force.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将hijacked定义为“由外力夺取对交通工具的控制权”。

q.en: Why did children in Hawaii make leis (flower necklaces)?
q.zh: 夏威夷的孩子们为什么要制作花环（鲜花项链）？

options.en: To send messages of hope and support to survivors and rescue workers
options.zh: 向幸存者和救援人员传递希望与支持的信息

options.en: For a birthday party according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了生日聚会（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says children in Hawaii made leis 'with messages written on them' and sent them to survivors and rescue workers at all three sites.
explain.zh: 书中说，夏威夷的孩子们制作了“写有信息的花环”，并寄给三个地点的幸存者和救援人员。

q.en: How many names are carved around the pools at the New York memorial?
q.zh: 纽约纪念广场的水池周围刻有多少个名字？

options.en: 184 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The book says 2,983 names are carved—victims of the three attacks plus the 1993 World Trade Center attack.
explain.zh: 书中说刻有2,983个名字——包括三次袭击的遇难者以及1993年世贸中心袭击的遇难者。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To teach about the attacks and how people remember and honor the victims
options.zh: 介绍袭击事件，以及人们如何纪念和缅怀遇难者

options.en: To argue against building memorials according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 反对建造纪念碑（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book describes the attacks, the heroes, and the many memorials created so people will 'never forget.'
explain.zh: 本书描述了袭击、英雄人物，以及众多纪念碑，目的是让人们“永不遗忘”。

q.en: What does the word 'memorial' mean as used in this book?
q.zh: 在本书中，“memorial”（纪念碑/纪念物）一词是什么意思？

options.en: A government building according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 政府大楼（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Something that represents the memory of a person, place, thing, or event
options.zh: 代表对某个人、地方、事物或事件的记忆的东西

explain.en: The glossary defines a memorial as 'something that represents the memory of a person, place, thing, or event.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将memorial定义为“代表对某个人、地方、事物或事件的记忆的东西”。

q.en: How is the Pentagon memorial different from the Pennsylvania memorial?
q.zh: 五角大楼纪念碑与宾夕法尼亚纪念碑有何不同？

options.en: The Pentagon has a wall of 40 marble pieces according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 五角大楼有一面由40块大理石组成的墙（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: The Pentagon has 184 benches with victims' names; Pennsylvania has a marble wall of 40 pieces with names
options.zh: 五角大楼有184张刻有遇难者名字的长椅；宾夕法尼亚有一面由40块大理石组成的刻名墙

explain.en: The book says the Pentagon has 184 benches (one per victim), while Pennsylvania's wall has 40 marble pieces (one per passenger).
explain.zh: 书中说五角大楼有184张长椅（每位遇难者一张），而宾夕法尼亚的墙由40块大理石组成（每位乘客一块）。

q.en: On what date did the New York City memorial open?
q.zh: 纽约市的纪念碑是在哪一天开放的？

options.en: September 11, 2008 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The book says the memorial 'opened on September 11, 2011' after nearly seven years of building.
explain.zh: 书中说，该纪念碑在建造近七年后“于2011年9月11日开放”。

q.en: What does the author say about remembering?
q.zh: 关于铭记，作者说了什么？

options.en: It is very important we never forget what happened and the heroes
options.zh: 我们永远不应忘记所发生的事以及那些英雄，这非常重要

options.en: We should forget to move on
options.zh: 我们应该忘记以继续前进

options.en: Only Americans should remember according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 只有美国人应该铭记（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says, 'It is very important that we never forget what happened, the people who died, and the people who became heroes.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“我们永远不应忘记所发生的事、逝去的人，以及那些成为英雄的人，这一点非常重要。”

prompt.en: The book shows many ways people remember September 11. Design your own memorial and explain what it would represent.
prompt.zh: 书中展示了人们纪念9月11日的多种方式。设计你自己的纪念碑，并说明它代表什么。

en: Describe what your memorial looks like.
Explain whose memory it honors and why.
zh: 解释它纪念的是谁，以及原因。
